info.name: Auth – Verify Email (POST /api/v1/auth/verify-email + POST /api/v1/auth/resend-verification)
info.description:
E2E tests for POST /api/v1/auth/verify-email and POST /api/v1/auth/resend-verification.

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-verify-email):
  1. setup             — register a fresh user, fetch OTP from Gmail, verify email
  2. validation        — all 422/413 cases for POST /verify-email
  3. happy-path        — OTP replay, indirect DB check via login, idempotent already-verified
  4. anti-enumeration  — unknown email and wrong-code return identical 422
  5. resend-validation — all 422/413 cases for POST /resend-verification
  6. resend-happy-path — 202 success + anti-enumeration paths
  7. rate-limiting     — IP and per-email limiters for both endpoints

All test users use @e2e.test emails so `make _e2e-clean` can wipe them with a single DELETE.

Notes on what is NOT covered (requires time manipulation, DB introspection, or fault injection):
  - ErrTokenExpired (needs fast-forwarding expires_at)
  - too_many_attempts from hitting max-attempts ceiling
  - account_locked (423) path
  - Exponential backoff gate (vfy:bo:)
  - Token revocation side-effects (RevokePreVerificationTokens, InvalidateAllUserTokens)
  - email_verified audit row
  - Resend cooldown guard (2-minute window)
  - Resend DB side-effects (fresh token row)
  - SMTP delivery failure → 503
  - Response timing invariants (±100 ms)

exec: // Set a unique IP in _xff before every request. Each request declares
// X-Forwarded-For: {{_xff}} so the server keys the rate-limit bucket on
// this IP rather than 127.0.0.1. This lets all non-RL folders run in a
// single Newman invocation without exhausting any limiter bucket.
// The rate-limiting folder overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 via a folder-level
// prerequest so all warmup requests there share one bucket and exhaust it.

item.name: setup
item.description: Registers a fresh user, fetches the OTP from Gmail, and verifies the email. Later folders depend on the verified account stored in vfy_happy_email / vfy_happy_password and the consumed OTP in 'code'.

exec: // Use the real Gmail address from the environment so the verification email
// is actually delivered to a live inbox that gmail_access_token can read.
// The address is fixed (not timestamp-unique) — _e2e-db-clean deletes it via
// E2E_GMAIL_EMAIL in .env before each run so this register never 409 on re-runs.
if (!email) { console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json (the Gmail address that receives SMTP mail).'); }
// Placed in the TEST script (not a prerequest) because pm.sendRequest is
// async. In a prerequest the JS thread blocks before the callback can fire.
// In a test script, the callback executes during the --delay-request idle
// window before the next request fires, so 'code' is set in time.
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API (async)...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in email body.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in collection variable "code":', match[1]);
// Validate that the Gmail fetch in the register step completed successfully.
// If 'code' is empty the verify request will fail with a validation error
// rather than a 200, making the root cause hard to diagnose.
console.error('[ERROR] collection variable "code" is empty — Gmail fetch in setup/register did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');

item.name: validation
item.description: All input-validation cases for POST /verify-email. Validation fires before any DB lookup so a non-existent email (vfy-val@e2e.test) is safe to use throughout — no row will be created.

exec: // Pad the body past the 1 MiB MaxBytesReader limit.
// http.MaxBytesReader fires during json.Decoder.Decode and maps to 413.

item.name: happy-path
item.description: Uses vfy_happy_email which was verified by the setup folder. The OTP in {{code}} was already consumed by setup.

exec: // Setup folder already consumed this OTP; replaying it must return 422,
// not 200. ErrTokenAlreadyUsed is mapped to validation_error — no hint
// is given about whether the token existed.
// After successful verification, login must return 200 — not 403
// email_not_verified. This indirectly confirms email_verified=true in DB.
// Email is normalised to lowercase before lookup — UPPERCASE resolves to
// the same account. The OTP was consumed by setup so the server returns
// 422 validation_error (token not found / already used) rather than 200.
// The server does not have a separate idempotent already-verified 200 path;
// email_verified=true is confirmed indirectly by the login test above.

item.name: anti-enumeration
item.description: Both cases must return identical 422 validation_error. No 404 is ever returned — the server cannot hint whether the email address is registered.

exec: // ErrTokenNotFound is mapped identically to a wrong-code response so
// the client cannot distinguish 'email not registered' from 'wrong OTP'.

item.name: wrong OTP (known email, below max-attempts) → 422 validation_error

exec: // Same status and body as the unknown-email case — client cannot
// distinguish the two responses and cannot enumerate account existence.

item.name: resend-validation
item.description: All input-validation cases for POST /resend-verification. Validation fires before any DB lookup.

exec: // Pad the body past the 1 MiB MaxBytesReader limit.

item.name: resend-happy-path
item.description: Anti-enumeration contract: every path returns 202 with the same message regardless of whether the email is registered, unverified, already verified, or unknown.

item.name: POST /register (setup unverified user for resend test)

exec: // Register a fresh unverified user to exercise the real resend path.
// No Gmail fetch needed — the next request only checks the 202 shape.
pm.test('message includes "if that email is registered"', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include('if that email is registered'));
// Service short-circuits on email_verified=true; response is identical
// to the happy path — no hint is given that the account is already verified.
pm.test('message includes "if that email is registered"', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include('if that email is registered'));
// No token row created, no audit row written; response is identical
// to the registered-user paths — client cannot distinguish them.
pm.test('message includes "if that email is registered"', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include('if that email is registered'));

item.name: rate-limiting
item.description: Verifies IP rate limiters for both endpoints. The folder prerequest overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all requests share one IP bucket. Section A: vfy:ip: (burst=5, rate=5/10min). Section B: rsnd:ip: (burst=3, rate=3/10min).

exec: // Override the collection-level unique IP so every request in this
// folder uses 127.0.0.1 as the client IP. All warmup requests share

item.name: warmup 5/5 — consume slot (→ not 429, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (rsnd:ip: slot 3 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest; overridden to 127.0.0.1 by the rate-limiting folder prerequest.
Set by setup folder. Reused across happy-path, anti-enumeration, and resend folders.
Set by setup folder. Used for the indirect login check in happy-path.
OTP fetched from Gmail. Set asynchronously by the setup → register test script during the --delay-request window.
